articles.title: Microsoft reports sinking Xbox revenue as its cloud business climbs
articles.description:
Microsoft's Xbox hardware revenue continues to tumble, with the company revealing a 33 percent decline as part of its earnings report released on Wednesday. Even though the rest of Microsoft's consumer-focused division took a dip, the company's cloud and productivity businesses continue to soar, driving the company toward $82.9 billion in revenue.
Along with declining Xbox hardware revenue, Microsoft reported a 5 percent drop in Xbox content and services as well. Microsoft has seen a significant amount of executive turnover over the past few months, with the retirement of Xbox chief CEO Phil Spencer and the departure of former Xbox presiden …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Kimmel reacts sharply to Trump’s ‘we won’t match that record’ marriage joke during King Charles event

Turning to first lady Melania Trump, he added: “That’s a record we won’t be able to match, darling, I’m sorry. Just not going to work out that way.”

articles.title: Google Search queries hit an ‘all time high’ last quarter
articles.description:
Google Search queries hit an "all time high" in the first quarter of 2026, according to a statement from CEO Sundar Pichai published as part of Alphabet's earnings on Wednesday.
"Our AI investments and full stack approach are lighting up every part of the business," Pichai says. "Search had a strong quarter with AI experiences driving usage, queries at an all time high, and 19% revenue growth." He also notes that Q1 was "our strongest quarter ever for our consumer AI plans, driven by the Gemini App" and that the company now has more than 350 million paid subscriptions, with "YouTube and Google One being the key drivers."
Alphabet also anno …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: 92.6% turnout in Phase 2 takes Bengal total to record 92.9%
articles.description: West Bengal witnessed an unprecedented voter turnout of 92.9% across two phases, surpassing previous records and nearing India's highest. This surge, particularly in Kolkata, is attributed to voter roll corrections and enhanced security. Despite a reduced electorate, more votes were cast, with women outvoting men. Officials are investigating complaints of vote tampering.

articles.title: Google parent Alphabet's cloud unit beats quarterly revenue estimates on strong AI demand
articles.description: Alphabet's cloud unit revenue surged 63% to $20 billion, exceeding estimates and driven by strong enterprise spending on AI infrastructure. The company's overall revenue also beat expectations, with its cloud backlog nearly doubling. This performance highlights Alphabet's significant gains in the competitive AI market, bolstered by its Gemini models and a key partnership with Apple.

articles.title: US stocks today: Microsoft cloud revenue accelerates as spending growth cools
articles.description: Microsoft's cloud revenue saw a jump in the March quarter. Spending on infrastructure was lower than anticipated. This performance aims to reassure investors about the company's significant artificial intelligence investments. Microsoft is expanding its AI offerings, including partnerships, to maintain its competitive edge in the cloud market. This comes as major tech firms invest heavily in AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Meta lifts capital expenditure forecast, doubling down on AI push
articles.description: Meta Platforms is significantly increasing its capital expenditure forecast to between $125 billion and $145 billion by 2026, doubling down on AI infrastructure investments despite planned layoffs. articles.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
articles.description:
The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents are circulating from the earliest days of OpenAI - and from before the AI lab even had a name. Some high-level takeaways: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang gave OpenAI an in-demand supercomputer, Musk largely drafted OpenAI's mission and heavily influenced its early structure, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman appeared to want to lean heavily on Y Combinator for early support for OpenAI, OpenAI president Greg Brockman and Ilya Sutskever worried about Musk's level of con …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Ubuntu’s AI plans have Linux users looking for a ‘kill switch’
articles.description:
Canonical's plan to add AI features to Ubuntu has some users asking for "a version of Ubuntu that does not include these features," while others say they'll stick with older versions of the Linux distro or even switch to a different one. After Canonical's announcement earlier this week that it's bringing AI features to Ubuntu, replies included requests for an AI "kill switch" or a way to disable the upcoming features, and comparisons to Microsoft's addition of AI features into Windows 11. Canonical's VP of engineering, Jon Seager, responded on Tuesday, stating that Canonical isn't planning to add a "global AI kill switch," but users will be  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Motorola just revealed the Razr Fold’s price and hoo boy
articles.description:
Gotta hand it to Motorola: it’s a good-looking phone.	

2026 is shaping up to be a tough year to launch a high-end phone. The memory crisis has phone prices rising across the board, so an already expensive phone risks becoming a much too expensive phone. That might be what happened to the Razr Fold, which will cost $1,900 when it goes on sale in the US on May 14th. That doesn't include the Moto Pen Ultra stylus, which costs an additional $100.
Motorola has dripped out some of the Fold's specs since it was first previewed at CES, and those early signs indicated that this device wouldn't be the more affordable folding phone the market so desperately needs. Today's announcement confirms that: it'll …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Supreme Court limits Voting Rights Act in setback for Black Democrats
articles.description: The Supreme Court retreats from 1986 rules that called for electing Blacks and Latinos to Congress and state and local boards.

articles.title: 5 things to know about the Supreme Court's landmark decision on the Voting Rights Act
articles.description: The Supreme Court, in Louisiana v. Callais, struck down Louisiana's second majority-Black congressional district.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund pulling the plug on LIV Golf funding after four years: report
articles.description: The Wall Street Journal reported that Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund is pulling funding from LIV Golf after four years of bankrolling the league.

articles.title: Supreme Court weakens Voting Rights Act, voiding Louisiana congressional map
articles.description: The Supreme Court rule 6-3 in a decision that has implications for the scope of the landmark Voting Rights Act.

articles.title: DOD officials say Iran war has cost $25 billion so far during Congressional grilling
articles.description: The Pentagon says that the cost of the war with Iran is estimated to be some $25 billion. Defense officials were appearing on the Hill for budget discussions.

articles.title: Iran War has cost the U.S. $25 billion so far, Pentagon official says
articles.description: A top Pentagon official on Wednesday said that the cost of the ongoing U.S. conflict with Iran is estimated to be $25 billion so far, as the war nears a 60-day mark

articles.title: Ford CFO on Q1 Earnings, Outlook and Impact of Gas Prices
articles.description: Ford CFO Sherry House discusses first-quarter earnings, SUV and pick-up truck sales amid higher gas prices and the automaker's full-year outlook on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Talks to bail out Spirit Airlines stall as company teeters toward collapse
articles.description: Spirit Airlines only has enough available cash to continue operations for a matter of days, not weeks, and talks for a government-backed rescue of the no-frills carrier have stalled, sources say.

articles.title: India’s Homegrown $1 Billion High-Speed Trading Unicorn Goes Global
articles.description: Graviton is expanding in Singapore and London as competition and regulatory hurdles build in its home market.

articles.title: Google gains 25M subscriptions in Q1, driven by YouTube and Google One
articles.description: Google added 25M paid subscriptions in Q1, reaching 350M total, as YouTube and Google One grow.

articles.title: FBI reveals Brown University mass shooter began plotting massacre as early as 2022, blamed victims
articles.description: The FBI concluded its investigation into the Brown University mass shooting and MIT professor murder, revealing the gunman began plotting in 2022.

articles.description: The Wall Street Journal reported that Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund is pulling funding from LIV Golf after four years of bankrolling the league.

topStories.description: Ukraine has perfected demining techniques in the Black Sea over four years of war against Russia.

topStories.description: Senators voted along partisan lines, with Republicans backing Warsh's nomination to lead the Fed and Democrats on the panel opposing him.

topStories.title: Emergency First Responders Say Waymos Are Getting Worse
topStories.description: “I believe the technology was deployed too quickly in too vast amounts, with hundreds of vehicles, when it wasn’t really ready,” one police official told federal regulators last month.

topStories.title: Kohberger defense team says it is 'appalled' by former expert's public comments about Idaho murders case
topStories.description: Bryan Kohberger's defense team says former expert Brent Turvey broke his confidentiality agreement by speaking publicly about the Idaho murders case. He denies wrongdoing.

topStories.title: Brady Tkachuk addresses trade rumors (again) after Senators playoff exit
topStories.description: Senators captain Brady Tkachuk expresses frustration over persistent trade rumors after Ottawa's first-round sweep by the Carolina Hurricanes.

topStories.title: Katie Porter's 'F*** Trump' email day after assassination attempt draws sharp rebuke: 'Degenerate loser'
topStories.description: California gubernatorial candidate Katie Porter faces criticism for a donor email containing "F*** Trump" sent a day after a Trump assassination attempt.

topStories.title: Kimmel reacts sharply to Trump’s ‘we won’t match that record’ marriage joke during King Charles event
topStories.description: During a formal reception welcoming King Charles, Trump reflected on his parents’ long marriage, noting they had been together for 63 years. Turning to first lady Melania Trump, he added: “That’s a record we won’t be able to match, darling, I’m sorry. Just not going to work out that way.”

dailyNews.title: Mamdani vs. Griffin Standoff Puts $6 Billion Tower at Risk
dailyNews.description: Citadel’s Ken Griffin said he’s meeting with New York Governor Kathy Hochul on Thursday to talk about the future direction of America’s biggest city after a spat with Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s over Griffin's $238 million Manhattan penthouse. (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: NFL indefinitely bans YouTuber who walked onto draft stage and grabbed the mic during Steelers' pick
dailyNews.description: YouTuber Fred Beyer earned an indefinite ban from all NFL events after crashing the 2026 NFL Draft stage in Pittsburgh with a fake credential.

dailyNews.title: Chipotle posts surprise same-store sales growth in early sign chain could be breaking its slump
dailyNews.description: Chipotle shares have plummeted 35% over the last year.

dailyNews.title: US stocks today: Microsoft cloud revenue accelerates as spending growth cools
dailyNews.description: Microsoft's cloud revenue saw a jump in the March quarter. Spending on infrastructure was lower than anticipated. This performance aims to reassure investors about the company's significant artificial intelligence investments. Microsoft is expanding its AI offerings, including partnerships, to maintain its competitive edge in the cloud market. This comes as major tech firms invest heavily in AI infrastructure.

dailyNews.title: Live Updates: King Charles and Queen Camilla in NYC
dailyNews.description: As part of his four-day trip to the U.S. to commemorate America's 250th birthday, King Charles III is taking part in a series of events in New York City.

dailyNews.title: Ford raises 2026 guidance as $1.3 billion tariff refund assists in offsetting higher costs
dailyNews.description: Aside from earnings and any changes to the automaker's 2026 guidance, investors will be monitoring effects from the Iran war, tariffs and the pullback in EVs.

dailyNews.title: Hegseth clashes with Democrats over Iran war, dismissal of top military leaders
dailyNews.description: The defense secretary sought to rebut lawmakers’ criticism of the Trump administration’s handling of the conflict, which has cost about $25 billion so far.

dailyNews.title: Alphabet earnings are out — here are the numbers
dailyNews.description: Alphabet reported first-quarter earnings after the bell Wednesday.

dailyNews.title: Ex-FBI chief Comey appears in court over alleged Trump threat
dailyNews.description: Comey turned himself in on two charges, threatening the US president's life and transmitting threats across state lines.

dailyNews.title: Truck driver who went missing in possible hijacking found dead: FBI
dailyNews.description: Alejandro Jacomino Gonzalez went missing while transporting vehicles from Georgia to Florida.

dailyNews.title: Johnson quells House GOP revolt to advance controversial surveillance bill
dailyNews.description: For more than two hours on the chamber’s floor, the speaker and fellow Republican leaders cajoled holdouts on that bill and others on DHS funding and farm policy.

dailyNews.title: Pentagon Puts Iran War Cost at $25 Billion as Hegseth Berates Skeptics
dailyNews.description: During his first public appearance on Capitol Hill since the war began, the defense secretary lashed out at lawmakers in both parties who have questioned the conflict.

topStories.title: Trump says he told Putin to end Ukraine war, not focus on Iran's uranium enrichment
topStories.description: Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Trump described the call as productive and said the two leaders discussed multiple global issues, with Ukraine remaining the central focus.

topStories.title: ABC can beat Trump FCC's license threat if owner Disney is willing to fight
topStories.description: Broadcast license renewals are "all but automatic" due to 1996 change in US law.

topStories.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Trump effort to end temporary protections for migrants
topStories.description: Conservative justices appear sympathetic to the administration’s contention that it can cancel humanitarian protections for Haitian and Syrian nationals.

topStories.title: WATCH:  Trump hosts Artemis II crew at White House after historic lunar flyby mission
topStories.description: President Trump welcomed the Artemis II crew to the White House after they completed a record-breaking trip around the moon.

topStories.title: Florida lawmakers pass a voting map that could help Republicans flip four House seats
topStories.description: The map drawn by Florida Governor Ron DeSantis boosts President Trump's effort to reshape voting before the midterm elections. The GOP likely holds a slight edge over Democrats in redistricting now.

topStories.title: Jerome Powell calls his time as Fed chair a 'privilege' during last press conference
topStories.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said his time as head of the central bank has been a "privilege," as he gave his last press conference as chair.  The Senate Banking Committee advanced President Donald Trump’s nominee to succeed Powell, Kevin Warsh. Powell will remain at the Fed after May 15, when his tenure as its chair ends.

topStories.title: Supreme Court leans in favor of Trump's bid to end protections for Syrian, Haitian migrants
topStories.description: Trump's lawyers say Congress gave the Department of Homeland Security, not judges, the power to end Temporary Protective Status granted earlier.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s $1 million ‘gold card’ visa has not been as popular as advertised
dailyNews.description: Just 165 people have paid the application fee so far, the government said in a new legal filing.

dailyNews.title: Pentagon to send one of its aircraft carriers home from Middle East
dailyNews.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, at sea for 10 months, is in need of repair. Its exit, though, reduces the firepower on hand as Trump presses Tehran to make peace.

dailyNews.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump in Instagram post
dailyNews.description: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

dailyNews.title: How Trump's EPA head has transformed the agency — and sided with polluters
dailyNews.description: New Yorker writer Elizabeth Kolbert says EPA chief Lee Zeldin has rescinded regulations, cut or eliminated departments and terminated the jobs of many scientists. Trump calls Zeldin "our secret weapon."

dailyNews.title: Alina Habba battles 'The View' over Comey prosecution in tense appearance
dailyNews.description: Former Trump administration advisor Alina Habba sparred with "The View" co-hosts over the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey and his "86 47" post.

dailyNews.title: 'America First' immigration overhaul bill would codify Trump's campaign promises once and for all
dailyNews.description: A new House Republican bill seeks to narrow legal immigration by ending the diversity visa lottery and shifting to a merit-based points system.

dailyNews.title: Trump ally James Comer defends Kimmel's free speech after 'widow' joke, says we shouldn't cancel comedy
dailyNews.description: Rep. James Comer disagreed with President Donald Trump on firing Jimmy Kimmel, defending free speech while calling the late-night host's Melania Trump joke tasteless.

dailyNews.title: 'Shark Tank' star backs Trump's White House ballroom plan amid security concerns: ‘It’s bipartisan’
dailyNews.description: "Shark Tank" star Kevin O'Leary supports Trump's White House ballroom plan, citing security concerns after the Correspondents' Dinner shooting.

dailyNews.title: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
dailyNews.description: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”

dailyNews.title: Melania Trump, Queen Camilla and the Look of the Special Relationship
dailyNews.description: Parsing a state visit told in photo ops, and style.

dailyNews.title: White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooter Planned Attack Weeks Before Gala, Prosecutors Say
dailyNews.description: A new prosecution memo details the preparations made in the days and weeks before the assault on the White House correspondents’ dinner.

dailyNews.title: All Six Conservative Supreme Court Justices Attended Trump’s Dinner With King Charles
dailyNews.description: Their appearance seemed at odds with the chief justice’s oft-stated message that the court he leads avoids even the appearance of political splits.
